instructions: Mix apples, brown sugar, cinnamon, lemon juice, apple cider, and salt together in a large pot over medium-high heat. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for until apples are soft, about 20 minutes. Use a potato masher while cooking to help break down the apples. Serve warm or cool.

dougs-crawfish-pie.name: Doug's Crawfish Pie

ingredients.item: Cooked and peeled whole crawfish tails
Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Melt butter in a large skillet on medium heat, then stir in flour; cook until light brown, about 10 minutes. Add sweet onion, green pepper, celery, and green onions. Cover and simmer on low heat for about one hour, stirring occasionally.

Stir in vegetable broth and tomato paste, and cook until it reaches a creamy consistency. Mix in crawfish tails, garlic, and parsley, then cook for about 15 minutes or until the tails are tender. Stir in salt, black pepper, cayenne pepper, and hot sauce.

Preheat an oven to 204°C (200 degrees C). Bake the puff pastry shells for 5 to 7 minutes, or until they puff up and start to brown a little. Remove from oven and set aside to cool. Pull off center tops and save. Turn oven down to 177°C (175 degrees C).

Spoon mixture into puff pastry shells until slightly overfilled. Put extra filling into a pie dish and cover with the remaining puff pastry shell center tops to create 6 small pies and 1 large crawfish pie. Place pies on a baking sheet.
Bake for about 15 minutes, or until the shells are golden brown and the filling has a nice shine to it.

dough-for-a-sweet-tart-argiro.name: Dough For A Sweet Tart

ingredients.item: 100 grbutyre (not too frozen, cut into cubes)
100 grbutyre (not too frozen, cut into cubes)

instructions: Shake the flour with the icing on the kitchen counter first.
Add the butter and zest and drizzle with the fingertips to become like wet sand.
Add the yolk and some water, 1-2 tablespoons, to combine the dough.
We give it a spherical shape and wrap it in membrane.
Let her rest and freeze for at least 30 minutes.
Spread the dough into a leaf and spread it into a buttered tart.
Fill to the rim of the tart with weights (paint, rice, etc.). and bake for 15-20 minutes.
Remove the weights and bake for 5-10 minutes to brown.
Brush it with egg or white solution to waterproof.
Bake for 3-4 more minutes.
Let it cool and continue with the cream we have chosen.

dough-for-cheesecakes-argiro.name: Dough For Cheesecakes
instructions: Mix in a bowl with a spoon the lukewarm milk, lukewarm water, yeast and sugar. Cover the bowl and allow it to foam and activate the yeast for 5 minutes.
Beat eggs with melted butter (or oil) and add them to the mixture of the activated yeast by mixing with a fork.
Include the flour and salt and mix until the ingredients are collected in a ball.
Dip the fingers in the soft butter and work the dough until it becomes smooth, soft and supple.
Transfer to a large clean bowl, cover it and leave it for one hour at room temperature to inflate enough.

When it doubles in volume, we pull it with fingers from the edges and pour it onto the counter. Attention, we're not flouring! Only a little oil or butter on the hands is enough to work the dough properly if necessary.

Form the dough into a long cord which is cut into 12 pieces. Create each piece of dough in a ball and close the compounds below. If we have time, cover the dough balls and let them rest for 15 minutes.
Press with the hands of each piece of dough to spread into a leaf and give it the oval shape.
Fill with some filling of our choice (salmy or sweet) and wrap until the middle of the leaf.
Cut the edges into strips and continue wrapping.
Add the pits to a baking pan lined with non-stick paper, spread them with egg yolk and sprinkle with sesame.
Cover the pits and let them stand for about 20 minutes and re-inflate.
Bake them in a well preheated oven at 180°C, in the air for 25 minutes, to brown and puff nicely.

dough-for-kourou-cheese-pies-with-yogurt.name: Dough For Kourou Cheese Pies With Yogurt
dough-for-kourou-cheese-pies-with-yogurt.original_name: Ζύμη για τυροπιτάκια κουρού με γιαούρτι
instructions: Add the flour to a bowl, puddle and add the soft butter, yoghurt, egg, salt, and kefalotiri.
Mix with a fork at the center taking the flour in the liquids a little bit.
After the materials are joined into dough and clean the bowl walls, we remove the rag dough on the counter.
The dough shouldn't stick to the hands. It must be malleable, fluffy, and soft.
We split in two equal places.
Make two dough balls, press lightly with the palms to become plates and wrap them in film or non-stick paper.
Place them in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes, so that the butter is well frozen so the dough can rest and we can open the leaf.
Open on a floured surface the two leaves with a thick molder. Fill with the filling of our liking, depending on the pie we make.

dough-for-piroski-argiro.name: Dough For Piroski
instructions: In a bowl, add the flour that inflates itself and the yeast.
In another bowl, beat the milk, sugar, salt, egg yolk and butter.
Add all the ingredients to the flour bowl.
Mix the ingredients until collected in a ball.
Work the dough on a floured surface until it becomes smooth, soft and supple.
Place it in a bowl, cover it and allow it to rest and double in volume, in a lukewarm spot, for about 45′.
After preparing the filling of our choice, empty the dough on the counter and press it to deflate.
We lightly ferment it.
Open a sheet with a thickness of 0.5 cm and cut discs with a diameter of 10 cm.
Add 1 tablespoon filling.
We're wetting the edges.
Close the compounds and roll the piroski in our hand, giving it a sausage shape.
We touch the piroskis on sheet coated with parchment paper, sparsely between them, because they swell.
After finishing, let them deflate for 15′-20′′.
Brush them with egg solution or milk and bake them in a well preheated oven at 170°C, for about 25′ until golden.
You can fill them with cheese, minced meat, sausage or potato.

dough-pie-of-epirus-argiro.name: Dough Pie Of Epirus
instructions: Place eggs in bowls first and whisk to dissolve.
Add the milk, olive oil, salt, pepper, feta cheese, flour and trahana.
Mix all the ingredients well.
We butter shallow large baking pan for pie.
Pour the thick porridge into a thin layer of 1 to 1.5 cm high.
From above we spread small pieces of butter.
Bake in a well preheated oven at 180 degrees for about 30 minutes until the surface is golden.

dough-pie-with-sun-dried-tomatoes-argiro.name: Dough Pie With Sun-dried Tomatoes
instructions: For easy pasta pie with smooth tomatoes, preheat the oven to 170°C, in the air.
Break the eggs in a bowl and beat with a hand mixer. Add the milk, salt, pepper and flour to form a thin porridge.
Place empty baking pan number 36 in the oven, warm for 7'-8'. In the meantime, cut the smooth tomatoes and keep 4 tablespoons of their oil.

Carefully remove (because it burns) the empty baking pan from the oven and brush with some olive oil well on the base and on the walls. Pour the dough in, sprinkle with feta cheese, smooth tomatoes and fresh oregano. Pour with the oil of the sundried tomato.
Bake the dough for about 50 minutes until golden.

dough-soup-lezzet.name: Dough Soup
instructions: 1. Noahs washed with plenty of water and süzün
2.  Üzerine su ekleyip 1 gece bekletin.
3. Change the water and pour it until it softens.
4. ÇThe dough of the forest is için, it is a rough and medium of the pool.
5. Salt and knead by adding some water.
6.  Hamurun üzerini nemli bezle örterek 10-15 dakika dinlendirin.
7. Spread the dough into the countertop;
8. Sewing yüksükükçsüüüük parçalarçboard and again wait in a sprinkled place.
9. Trap salt, adding peppers and nuts büyüklükü prepared köfteler in k&ouml.
10. melt the butter in the pan.
11. 11. Add and roast to Salç
12.  Su ilave edin.
12. August Add Küçük dough parçaler and köfte.
14 July Add cheeks and köftes thoroughly cooked chickpeas.
15. After 10 minutes add min, pepper and get from the stove.
16. Serve hot.

doughnut-bread-pudding.name: Doughnut Bread Pudding

ingredients.item: Stale or fresh glazed or sugared doughnuts

instructions: Place a rack in center of oven; preheat to 325°. Butter a 2-qt. baking dish. Cut each doughnut into eighths and arrange on a parchment-lined rimmed baking sheet. Bake until dry and crisp, like little sugary croutons, about 15 minutes. Let sit until cool enough to handle. (Drying out the doughnut pieces will help them absorb the custard.)

Vigorously whisk eggs, egg yolks, half-and-half, sugar, vanilla, salt, and lemon zest in a large bowl until light, uniform, and foamy. (You can also process in a blender until combined if you want.) Add doughnuts to bowl with custard. Gently toss and push down on them so that they absorb maximum liquid without falling apart. Let sit 5 minutes, then toss and press gently again. Repeat tossing and soaking twice more for a total soak time of 15 minutes, allowing the doughnuts to fully drink up the custard.

Transfer doughnuts and any remaining custard to prepared baking dish. Dollop preserves over, avoiding edges of pan where it can burn during baking. Bake bread pudding, rotating pan halfway through, until puffed, golden brown, and bubbling along the edges, 50–60 minutes.
Let bread pudding cool 20 minutes. Dust with powdered sugar. Serve with cream alongside for pouring over.
Do Ahead: Doughnuts can be baked 1 day ahead. Store in an airtight container at room temperature.

doughnut-puffs-from-land-olakes.name: Doughnut Puffs From Land O'lakes

ingredients.item: Land o lakes® butter with canola oil

instructions: Heat oven to 191°C. Grease 12-cup muffin pan; set aside.
Combine milk, 1/3 cup melted butter and egg in bowl. Set aside.
Combine flour, 3/4 cup sugar, baking powder, salt and nutmeg in bowl. Add milk mixture; stir just until combined.
Spoon batter into prepared pan. Bake 15-20 minutes or until toothpick inserted comes out clean.
Place 1/3 cup melted butter in bowl. Combine 1/2 cup sugar and cinnamon in another bowl. Remove muffins from pan; let stand 2 minutes on cooling rack.
Dip tops of warm muffins into melted butter, then into cinnamon sugar mixture.
Serve warm with Butter with Canola Oil.

doughnuts-with-grapefruit-curd-and-citru.name: Doughnuts With Grapefruit Curd And Citrus Sugar

ingredients.item: A 2 0.25–2 0.5-inch round cutter; a deep-fry thermometer

instructions: Bring grapefruit juice to a simmer in a small saucepan over medium-high heat and cook until reduced to ⅓ cup, 12–14 minutes. Let cool slightly.

Whisk egg yolks, eggs, sugar, and salt in a medium heatproof bowl to combine. Whisk in grapefruit zest, lemon zest, lemon juice, and reduced grapefruit juice. Set bowl over a large saucepan of simmering water. Cook, whisking almost constantly, until curd is thickened, 6–8 minutes. Remove from heat and whisk in butter, adding a few pieces at a time.
Transfer to a nonreactive bowl and press a piece of plastic wrap directly onto surface of curd. Chill until set up and very cold, at least 4 hours and up to 12 hours.

Combine sugar and zest in a small bowl. Let sit uncovered 12 hours to dry out. (You can also microwave on high in 15-second intervals, stirring and letting cool slightly between intervals, until dehydrated.)
Transfer to a blender and blend until powdery. (Or, work zest into sugar with your fingers; just make sure to break up any clumps.)
Do Ahead: Citrus sugar can be made 3 days ahead. Store airtight at room temperature.

Heat milk in a small saucepan or microwave until just warm (you want it to be between 105° and 110°). Transfer to the bowl of a stand mixer and scrape in vanilla seeds; reserve pod for another use. Add 1 Tbsp. sugar and sprinkle yeast over. Let sit until yeast starts to foam, about 5 minutes.

Add egg, egg yolk, honey, lemon zest, salt, and remaining 2 Tbsp. sugar. Mix gently with a wooden spoon until barely combined. Add 2¾ cups flour and knead with a dough hook on medium-high speed until dough comes together into a mass that no longer sticks to the sides of bowl and is beginning to climb up the hook, about 5 minutes. (Dough should spring back immediately when pressed.) Reduce speed to medium-low; add butter a couple of pieces at a time, working in completely after each addition.

Turn out dough onto a work surface and knead with your hands until smooth and springy, about 2 minutes. Transfer to a very lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let rise in a warm spot until doubled in volume, about 2 hours.

Turn out dough onto a lightly floured surface and roll out ½" thick. Cut out doughnuts with cutter, twisting cutter to release them. Reroll any scraps. Transfer to a very lightly floured parchment paper–lined baking sheet. Cover with a kitchen towel; let sit in a warm spot until slightly puffed and dough springs back lightly when pressed, 40–45 minutes.

Fit a wide heavy pot with thermometer; pour in oil to come at least 2" up sides. Heat over medium-high until thermometer registers 325°. Fry 3 or 4 doughnuts at a time, turning halfway through, until deep golden brown, about 6 minutes per batch. Transfer to a wire rack set inside a rimmed baking sheet; let cool.

Using a paring knife, poke a hole in the top of each doughnut, being careful not to push through to the opposite side. Transfer grapefruit curd to a piping bag or resealable plastic bag (snip off one corner) fitted with a ¼” round tip. Fill doughnuts with grapefruit curd, then coat generously with citrus sugar.

doughnuts-with-grapefruit-vanilla-jelly.name: Doughnuts With Grapefruit-vanilla Jelly
instructions: Combine sugar, grapefruit juice, and pectin in a large saucepan; scrape in seeds from vanilla bean. Cook over medium heat, stirring, until sugar dissolves. Increase heat to high; whisk until mixture boils vigorously, about 6 minutes. Pour mixture into a shallow glass baking dish. Let cool completely at room temperature. Cover, and chill until set, at least 2 hours. DO AHEAD: Can be made 1 week ahead. Keep chilled.

Lightly coat a large bowl with olive oil. Heat juice in a small saucepan over medium heat until an instant-read thermometer registers 110°–115°. Remove from heat; whisk in yeast. Let stand until mixture becomes foamy, about 10 minutes.

In a stand mixer fitted with a dough hook, mix 3 1/2 cups flour, 2 Tbsp. sugar, eggs, grapefruit zest, salt, 2 Tbsp. olive oil, and apple-juice mixture on medium speed until a sticky dough forms, 4–5 minutes. Transfer to oiled bowl. Turn to coat; cover with a kitchen towel. Let dough rise in a warm, draft-free place until doubled in size, about 1 hour.
Gently roll out dough on a floured surface to 1/2” thickness. Cut out rounds with biscuit cutter. Place on lightly floured baking sheets. Let rise until doubled, 30–40 minutes.

Place a wire rack inside a rimmed baking sheet; set aside. Pour remaining 1 cup sugar into a wide shallow bowl; set aside. Attach a deep-fry thermometer to side of a heavy pot. Pour vegetable oil into pot to a depth of 2" and heat over medium heat to 350°. Working in batches, carefully add doughnuts to oil. Fry until golden brown, about 2 minutes per side, allowing oil to return to 350° between batches. Using a slotted spoon, transfer doughnuts to rack; let drain for 2 minutes. Roll in sugar in bowl to coat.
Transfer grapefruit jelly to a pastry bag fitted with a 1/4" metal tip or squeeze bottle. Pipe a little more than 1 tsp. jelly into each doughnut. Serve immediately.

douro-style-stewed-octopus-teleculinaria.name: Douro-style Stewed Octopus
douro-style-stewed-octopus-teleculinaria.original_name: Polvo estufado à moda do Douro
instructions: Cut the octopus, still raw, into small pieces and boil them in water for a few minutes. Drain and reserve.
Make a braise with the olive oil, the laurel, the onion and the chopped garlic. Then add the octopus and let it cook a little.
Acrescente o tomate esmagado e deixe refogar. Regue com o vinho e deixe reduzir.
Add some water, season with salt and pepper, cover and let cooking until the octopus becomes soft. Serve sprinkled with chopped parsley and accompanied by cooked potatoes.

dovga-soup-lezzet.name: Dovga Soup
instructions: 1. Boil in the pot by adding rice, water and lumbar stems.
2. Add and mix the lugs into it when the brass and lumbar stems begin to pry.
3. Add the mint to the mixture after the thin sparkle of mint, dill and yeast.
4. After making hot water addition on it, cook for 10 minutes by adding nohut and salt.
5. Mix yogurt, flour and eggs in a bowl.
6. Add a bucket on the soup and spark the mixture with yogurt.
7. Then add this mixture slowly to the soup and mix.
8. You can serve the soup in a warm way.
9.  Afiyet olsun.

down-home-collard-greens.name: Down Home Collard Greens
instructions: Put collard greens in a pot full of lightly salted water; bring to a boil, reduce heat to medium-low, and cook until tender, about 1 hour. Drain.
Cook bacon in a large skillet over medium-high heat until crisp, about 10 minutes. Drain the bacon on paper towels.

Melt butter in a large pot over medium heat. Cook and stir onion in melted butter until tender, 7 to 10 minutes. Stir bacon, chicken bouillon, water, garlic, oregano, thyme, and savory into the onion; bring to a boil. Stir collard greens into the mixture and simmer 5 minutes. Pour milk over the mixture, stir, and cook another 5 minutes.

down-home-black-eyed-peas.name: Down-home Black-eyed Peas
instructions: Pick over peas, rinse, and place in a large Dutch oven or soup pot with water; bring to a boil for 2 minutes. Remove from heat, cover, and let stand for 1 hour.
Stir in ham hocks, onion, celery, salt, cayenne pepper, and bay leaf; bring to a boil, cover the pot, and simmer until ham hocks are tender, about 1 ½ hours.
Stir in okra and simmer until tender, 10 to 15 minutes. Remove and discard bay leaf before serving.

downeast-maine-pumpkin-bread.name: Downeast Maine Pumpkin Bread
instructions: Whisk flour, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, nutmeg, cloves, and ginger together in a large bowl.
Mix pumpkin puree, eggs, oil, water, and sugar in a separate bowl until well blended.
Stir flour mixture into pumpkin mixture until just blended.
Pour batter into the prepared pans.
Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted in center comes out clean, about 50 minutes.
Enjoy!

doy-doy-soup-recipe-lezzet.name: Doy Doy Soup Recipe

ingredients.item: Instead of Arpa city, you can use wire city or küçük cutting pasta.
You can prefer yellow lentils instead of red lentil.
You can add 1 tooth crushed garlic in the roasting phase of a dense aroma için.
Salça can only use pepper salça half spoon tomato salça + half spoon pepper salça.
çiçin küçüük küp chopped havuç or potato addition.
More dense consistency can increase the amount of için 1–2 tablespoons.
ü you can dilute the flavor by adding buttered mint or lemon juice while serving the car.

instructions: 1. Dry onions küçük küpler in form.
2. Take the olive oil and butter to the pot.
3. Roast until pink.
4. In addition to the pepper salç add the smellç until the breeze, continue to roast more minutes.
5. Add chopped red lentils and mix short süre.
6. Then add hot water and leave boiling.
7. Add the barley city when you start boiling.
8. Cook them in the middle fire until they soften.
9. A&ccedil with 1 water glass warm water in a separate bowl of dust tar.
10. Do not collect için stirringç add to theorbaya.
11. Tuz, pul biber ve kuru naneyi ilave edin.
12. boil 10–15 minutes more.
12. August You can adjust the container by adding hot water if it is dark.
14 July After closing the gold of the furnace, listen to 5 minutes and serve hot.
15.  Afiyet olsun.

dr-bird-cake.name: Dr. Bird Cake

ingredients.item: (1 0.75 cups plus 2 tbsp.) whole wheat flour or all-purpose flour

ingredients.prep: or oil of choice, plus extra for greasing

instructions: Preheat the oven to 204°C (180°C) and grease and line the base of a 9½ inch round cake tin. (If making a double-layered cake, use 2 tins and double the quantity of cake mixture and frosting).
Put the nuts and pineapple in a blender or food processor and pulse briefly until finely chopped.
Sift all the dry ingredients, up to and including the salt, into a mixing bowl, then stir until combined and set aside.

In a separate bowl, add the bananas, coconut oil or butter, reserved pineapple juice, vanilla, vinegar, and egg, if using. Whisk until combined, then add to the dry ingredients with the pecan and pineapple mix. Briefly stir until combined, but do not over-mix the cake batter.

Spoon the mixture into the cake tin and level the top. Bake for 30 minutes, or until a skewer inserted into the middle comes out clean. Leave to cool for 5 minutes, then turn out of the tin onto a rack to cool completely.

Meanwhile, make the frosting. Beat the butter and cream cheese together until light and fluffy. Add the salt and vanilla, then stir in the icing sugar, a little at a time, to make a thick, spreadable icing. Spread the frosting over the top of the cake (or half over each cake if making 2). Sprinkle with the coconut flakes and pecans.

dragans-leg-of-lamb-with-garlic-and-beer.name: Dragan's Leg Of Lamb With Garlic And Beer
instructions: Preheat the oven to 204°C (200 degrees C).
Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Add potatoes and carrots; cook in boiling water for about 3 minutes. Drain and set aside.

Rinse leg of lamb, pat dry, and place in a roasting pan. Season generously with salt and pepper; rub into meat. Use a small knife to make incisions big enough for a garlic clove to fit all over lamb. Stuff garlic cloves into the holes. Spoon mustard over lamb; rub into meat.

Roast, uncovered, in the preheated oven for about 30 minutes. Reduce oven temperature to 191°C (190 degrees C). Add potatoes and carrots to the roasting pan. Baste lamb with beer, reserving the rest for basting at 20-minute intervals.
Continue to roast lamb until the internal temperature reaches at least 60°C (60 degrees C) for medium rare, about 1 hour 30 minutes more.
Remove from the oven and allow to rest for at least 5 minutes before carving. Spoon pan drippings over meat and vegetables to serve.

dragon-deviled-eggs.name: Dragon Deviled Eggs
instructions: Place the eggs in a medium pot and fill with cold water until the eggs are just covered. Bring the water to a boil, then cover the pot, remove from the heat, and let sit for 10 minutes. Meanwhile, prepare an ice bath in a large bowl. Transfer the eggs to the ice bath and let sit for 3 minutes.
Use your hand to roll the eggs carefully on a flat surface to create cracks.

Place each cracked egg in a small zip-top bag and add a few drops of teal food coloring. Seal the bags and gently roll the eggs to coat evenly in the food coloring. Let sit for 30 minutes, then remove the eggs from the bags and rinse.
Remove the cracked eggshells from the eggs and cut in half lengthwise. Scoop the yolks into a medium bowl. Arrange the whites on a serving platter.
To the bowl with the yolks, add the mayonnaise, mustard, bacon, jalapeño, hot sauce, and salt to taste. Mix well to combine.
Scoop the egg yolk filling into the egg whites and garnish with paprika.
Enjoy!

dragon-fruit-mojito.name: Dragon Fruit Mojito
instructions: Mix 2 tablespoons plus 2 teaspoons sugar with mint in the bottom of a highball glass.
Combine rum and dragon fruit flesh in a blender; blend until smooth. Pour rum mixture over the muddled mint mixture. Stir to incorporate.
Divide mixture between 4 double old-fashioned glasses. Top each drink with equal amounts of club soda.

dragon-potatoes.name: Dragon Potatoes

ingredients.prep: stems removed, soaked in water and blended

instructions: For the black beans: Soak beans overnight.

Put the beans in a large stockpot and add 8 cups cold water. Bring to a boil, then add cumin, chili powder, oregano, basil, garlic, bay leaves, onion and salt. Cover and simmer until the beans are tender, about 1 1/2 hours.
For the carnitas: Preheat the oven to 177°C.

Cut pork into 4 or 5 pieces. Rub chili powder, cumin, oregano, salt, pepper and chile sauce all over the pork and place in a baking pan. Add the garlic, bay leaf and onion. Add 1 cup of water to the pan and cover with foil. Bake until meat is tender and will shred with a fork, 1 1/2 hours.
For the rosemary potatoes: Put potatoes in a pot of water and bring to a boil. Cook until tender when pierced with a fork, about 30 minutes. Drain the water and set the potatoes aside to cool.

When the potatoes are cool enough to touch, smash them with your hands to flatten them into patties. Heat half the margarine on a flat-top grill or griddle until melted, then add the smashed potatoes. Put the remaining margarine or butter on top of the potatoes and sprinkle with rosemary and black pepper, then cook until golden brown, about 6 minutes. Flip the potatoes, then cook until golden brown, an additional 6 minutes. (Keep the flat-top hot.)
For the avocado sauce: Put avocado flesh, cilantro, garlic and jalapeno in a blender. Blend while slowly adding milk of choice.

For the dragon potatoes: Divide potatoes among 5 plates. Cover with black beans and top with carnitas. Grill the jalapenos on the flat-top for 1 minute and place on top of carnitas. Top dish with jack cheese, tomatoes, green onions, cilantro, sour cream and avocado sauce.

dragon-roll.name: Dragon Roll
instructions: Rinse rice in a strainer until water runs clear. Combine rinsed rice and water in a medium saucepan; bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook until rice is tender and water is absorbed, about 20 minutes.
Combine rice vinegar, sugar, and salt in a small saucepan over low heat; stir until sugar is dissolved, 1 to 2 minutes. Pour over rice; stir until rice cools and looks dry.
Preheat the oven to 204°C (200 degrees C). Grease a baking sheet with cooking spray.
Arrange shrimp tempura on the prepared baking sheet.
Bake in the preheated oven until golden and crispy, about 6 minutes per side. Cut the tails off of 4 tempura shrimp.

Cover a bamboo sushi rolling mat with plastic wrap. Lay 1 nori sheet, shiny-side down, on the plastic wrap. Spread 1 cup rice over nori using moistened fingers, leaving a 1/3-inch border. Flip nori so rice is facing the mat.

Arrange imitation crabmeat and cucumber along the bottom edge of nori. Place 2 tail-off shrimp in the center of nori. Place 2 tail-on shrimp on the end so that the tails extend over the sides of nori. Lift the edge of the mat and roll up nori into a tight log around filling.

Transfer sushi roll to a serving plate. Layer avocado and unagi slices on top to cover the top and sides of roll. Slice roll into 8 pieces using a moistened knife. Repeat with remaining nori, rice, crabmeat, cucumber, avocado, and unagi to make a second roll.

drawn-butter.name: Drawn Butter
instructions: Melt butter in the top of a double boiler over barely simmering water, 5 to 7 minutes.
Set melted butter aside to cool slightly, then skim foam off the top and discard. Allow butter to solidify, 8 hours to overnight.
Drain off any liquid that is collected at the bottom. Transfer butter to a microwave-safe glass or ceramic bowl and microwave until melted. Pour into individual bowls to serve.

dream-recipe-teleculinaria.name: Dream Recipe
instructions: Prepare the syrup: light a pot with water, sugar, cinnamon stick and orange and lemon peels. Let it boil for 10 minutes, stirring from time to time. Remove from the fire and reserve.

Wipe the flour with a pinch of salt. Dissolve it in 150 ml of cold water. Bring to the fire a pot of 100 ml of water and when it starts boiling, add the butter. Add the dissolved flour and stir continuously. Remove from the fire when the dough is removed from the pan.
Let the dough cool a little, sprinkle with the yeast and stir well. Add the eggs one by one and stir between each addition. Hit the dough until you start making bubbles.

Bring a pot of oil to the fire and let it warm. Add spoonfuls of pasta and let them fry, in medium heat, until they gain color (tick with a fork, so they don't get badly cooked). Remove with a skimmer and let them drip on absorbent paper. Lay your dreams on a plate, water them with the syrup and serve them at room temperature.

dream-recipe-with-5-ingredients-teleculi.name: Dream Recipe With 5 Ingredients
instructions: Lay the water, the margarine and a pinch of salt in a pot and lead to the boiling heat. Add the flour, in rain and always stirring, until you get a ball of dough that takes off from the pot. Remove from the fire, transfer to a bowl and let it cool.
Add the eggs, one by one and kneading very well in a movement of opening and closing hands.
Heat a pot with abundant oil (without letting too much heat) and fry small spoonfuls of dough. Remove and drain on absorbent paper.
Involve some sugar and cinnamon powder and pass the dreams through this mixture. Serve right away.

dreams-filled-with-almond-cream-teleculi.name: Dreams Filled With Almond Cream
dreams-filled-with-almond-cream-teleculi.original_name: Sonhos recheados com creme de amêndoa

ingredients.item: 1 colher de sopa bem cheia de farinha maisena

instructions: Prepare the cream: Light a pot with water, sugar, lemon peel and cinnamon stick and let it boil for 5 minutes. Then add the almond powder, stir and let it boil for another 3 minutes. In a bowl, beat the yolks with the masena flour, add the previous mixture, in yarn and always stirring, pour it into the pot and take it back to the fire, stirring continuously until thicken. Remove from the fire, lay in a bowl, reject cinnamon and lemon and let it cool.

Light another pot with water, milk, margarine and a pinch of salt and let it boil. Then add the flour in "rain" and always stir until a ball is left and let the dough cook, stirring over and over for 5 minutes. Remove from the fire, lay in a bowl, cool down and then add the eggs, one by one and always kneading very well.

Heat a deep fryer with abundant oil, gather spoonfuls from the previous mixture and let them fry until they are well brown. Remove your dreams, let them run down a tray with kitchen paper and then make them a hole with the tip of a thin knife. Place the cream that you have prepared inside a bag of pastries with smooth and fine mouths, fill your dreams, powder them with sugar and serve them sprinkled with cinnamon and decorated to taste. It can also sprinkle with granulated almond.

dreams-in-lemon-syrup-teleculinaria.name: Dreams In Lemon Syrup
instructions: Light a pan with 400 ml of water, the margarine and a pinch of salt and let it boil. Then add the flour, in the form of rain, always stirring until you get a ball of dough that takes off from the pot. Remove it from the fire, put it in a bowl and let it cool.
Then add the eggs, one by one and always crushing very well in a movement of opening and closing hands.
Light a large frying pan with abundant oil and let it warm (but not too much). Add small spoonfuls of pasta and let it fry until the dreams are golden. Remove and drain on absorbent paper.

Place 300 ml of water in a pan and the other ingredients indicated for syrup, take it to the fire and boil for 3 minutes. Remove from the fire, lay on your dreams and serve sprinkled with powdered sugar.

dreams-of-chicken-and-beer-teleculinaria.name: Dreams Of Chicken And Beer
instructions: Wipe the chicken's breasts and legs, reject skins and bones and cut them into small pieces.

Take a fryer to the fire with oil and let it warm up. In a bowl, beat the eggs, add the flour, beer, garlic powder and chopped parsley, season with salt and pepper and wrap, without knocking, until it is homogeneous.

Pass the chicken pieces in the previous mixture and take them to fry, few at a time, between 3 and 5 minutes or until they are golden. Remove them, let them drip on a sheet of kitchen paper and then serve them. At work, go with salad to taste.

dreamsicle-iced-tea-latte.name: Dreamsicle Iced Tea Latte
instructions: Combine 1 cup water and brown sugar in a saucepan; bring to a boil, stirring frequently. Remove from heat; stir in almond extract and vanilla extract.
Combine remaining water and almond milk in a saucepan; bring to a boil. Add tea bags; steep for 5 minutes. Remove tea bags; stir in extract mixture. Fill two tall glasses with ice; pour in tea.

dressing.name: Dressing

ingredients.item: Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper

instructions: Tear bread into 1” pieces (you should have about 20 cups); spread out on baking sheets and let sit overnight to dry out.

Preheat oven to 375°. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add onions, celery, garlic, sage, and thyme; season with salt and pepper and cook, stirring occasionally, until vegetables are soft, 10–12 minutes. Add butter, celery seeds, cayenne, and broth and cook, stirring occasionally, until butter is melted.
Transfer to a large bowl; add bread and parsley. Toss, adding more broth as needed to moisten.
Transfer to a 13x9” baking dish; cover with foil. Bake until heated through, 45–50 minutes. Uncover; bake until dressing is browned and crisp, 15–20 minutes longer.

instructions: Flatten cranberries between fingers and spread them onto a sheet of waxed paper to dry until they start to curl slightly, about 1 hour.
Put cranberries into a bowl labeled 'Dried Bloody Scabs' and watch the kids scoop them up by the handful!

dried-cherry-and-cranberry-sauce.name: Dried Cherry And Cranberry Sauce
instructions: Stir the cranberry juice cocktail, water, brown sugar, cinnamon stick, and allspice together in a saucepan; bring to a boil. Add the cherries to the cranberry juice mixture; cook another 2 minutes. Add the cranberries; continue cooking, stirring occasionally, until the cranberries pop, about 10 minutes. Remove from heat and chill in refrigerator 8 hours to overnight. Remove and discard cinnamon stick before serving.

dried-cherry-and-pecan-fougasse.name: Dried Cherry And Pecan Fougasse

ingredients.item: Egg beaten to blend with 1 teaspoon water

instructions: Pour 1/2 cup warm water into 2-cup measuring cup. Sprinkle yeast, then 1 teaspoon sugar over; stir to blend. Let stand until yeast dissolves and mixture bubbles and looks creamy, 5 to 7 minutes. Add remaining 1 cup warm water, 3 tablespoons oil, and honey to yeast mixture.

Combine flour and salt in bowl of heavy-duty mixer; stir to blend. Pour in yeast mixture. Attach dough hook and beat at medium-low speed until flour is evenly moistened but looks shaggy, occasionally stirring flour into center, about 3 minutes. Increase mixer speed to medium and beat until dough pulls away from sides of bowl and climbs hook, about 10 minutes (dough will be soft and sticky like batter).
Combine cherries, pecans, and orange peel in medium bowl; toss to blend. Add to dough and beat 1 minute. Using sturdy spatula, stir dough by hand to blend completely.

Lightly oil large bowl. Scrape dough into bowl. Brush top of dough lightly with oil. Brush sheet of plastic wrap with oil and cover bowl, oiled side down. Let dough rise in warm draft-free area until doubled in volume, 1 to 2 hours.
Gently turn dough over several times with spatula to deflate. Re-cover bowl with oiled plastic; chill overnight (dough will rise).

Sprinkle 2 large rimmed baking sheets lightly with flour or line with parchment paper. Using spatula, deflate dough by stirring or folding over several times. Divide dough into 2 equal pieces. Place 1 piece on lightly floured work surface; sprinkle top with flour. Roll out dough to 12x8- to 12x9-inch rectangle, sprinkling lightly with flour as needed to keep from sticking. Transfer dough to prepared sheet.

Using very sharp small knife, cut four 2-inch-long diagonal slashes to right of center of dough rectangle and 4 more to left of center to create pattern resembling leaf veins. Pull slashes apart with fingertips to make 3/4- to 1-inch-wide openings.
Repeat with remaining dough. Cover dough on each sheet with towel. Let rest 20 minutes.
Position 1 rack in top third and 1 rack in bottom third of oven; preheat to 232°C. Brush each fougasse with egg glaze; sprinkle with raw sugar and pierce all over with fork.

Bake fougasses 10 minutes. Reverse position of baking sheets, top to bottom, and turn sheets around. Bake fougasses until crusts are deep golden brown, about 8 minutes longer. Transfer fougasses to racks and cool 15 minutes. Serve warm or at room temperature.

dried-cherry-bread.name: Dried Cherry Bread
instructions: Pour boiling water to cover dried cherries in a bowl; let sit to plump up for 15 minutes. Drain.
Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Grease and flour a 9x5-inch loaf pan.
Lightly beat eggs in a bowl; whisk in sugar, yogurt, oil, butter, buttermilk, and vanilla extract to combine.

Whisk flour, oatmeal, baking powder, baking soda, and salt together in a separate, larger bowl; add egg mixture, white chocolate chips, and cherries. Stir until just combined. Don't overmix. Pour into the prepared pan.

Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, 45 to 60 minutes, checking at 35 minutes to account for different ovens. If bread begins to brown too much, lightly cover the top with aluminum foil. Cool in the pan 5 to 10 minutes before inverting loaf onto a wire rack to cool completely.

dried-chile-salsa.name: Dried Chile Salsa
instructions: Toast chiles in a dry medium skillet over high heat, turning often, until fragrant, about 3 minutes. Let cool slightly, then remove stems and seeds. Place chiles, onion, garlic, salt, and 1 1/2 cups water in a medium saucepan. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer until very soft, 15–20 minutes. Purée chile mixture in a blender until smooth. Strain into a medium bowl and mix in vinegar.
DO AHEAD: Salsa can be made 4 days ahead. Cover and chill.

instructions: Place fava beans in medium bowl. Pour enough water over to cover by 2 inches. Cover and let soak at room temperature overnight. Drain fava beans. Peel off outer shell and skin from beans and discard.

Place fava beans in large saucepan. Add 8 cups water, potato, onion, and carrot and bring to boil, skimming off any foam that rises to surface with large spoon. Reduce heat to medium; add pinch of salt and simmer uncovered until beans are very soft, stirring occasionally and adding more boiling water as needed to keep beans submerged, about 1 hour 45 minutes. Drain. Transfer bean mixture to processor and puree until almost smooth. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Transfer fava bean puree to bowl.

Heat 2 tablespoons oil in large skillet over medium-high heat. Add dandelion greens, garlic, and crushed red pepper; sauté until greens wilt, about 2 minutes. Season to taste with salt and freshly ground black pepper. Place greens atop fava bean puree. Drizzle with remaining 1 tablespoon oil; sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
Keep a pot of boiling water on the stove so you can add boiling water to the beans as needed.

Bring mixture to boil. Reduce heat to low and simmer 2 minutes. Remove from heat. DO AHEAD Can be made 2 hours ahead. Let stand at room temperature.

Whisk flour, 2 tablespoons sugar, baking soda, and sea salt in medium bowl to blend. Add butter and rub in with fingertips until very coarse meal forms (butter pieces will be reduced to size of small peas). Add buttermilk and stir just until dough comes together (dough will be slightly wet). Mix remaining 2 tablespoons sugar and cinnamon in small bowl to blend.

Return dried fruit mixture to simmer. Using generous 1/4 cup biscuit dough for each, scoop 8 mounds of dough atop simmering fruit mixture, spacing apart. Sprinkle cinnamon sugar over each biscuit. Cover skillet tightly and simmer over medium-low heat until biscuits are puffy and cooked through (tester inserted into center of biscuits comes out clean), about 12 minutes. Remove skillet from heat. Let slump stand uncovered 15 minutes.

Meanwhile position rack in upper third of oven at least 6 inches from heat source and preheat broiler. Broil slump just until biscuit tops are light golden, watching closely to prevent burning, 1 to 2 minutes.
Divide fruit slump among bowls. Pour chilled cream over each and serve.

instructions: Prepare the base mass: Lay the yeast on the robot cup and add 50 g of warm milk. Add the flour, butter, sugar and eggs and turn on 1 minute at the special speed for pasta. Split the dough into two equal parts. Prepare the braid: add the sultans and half the almond to one of the masses and wrap it very well. Split it into three equal parts, mold 3 rolls, line them up and form a braid. Let it take another 15 minutes.

Prepare the cake-king: in the other dough, incorporate the sausage and ham, previously cut into cubes. Divide into two parts, mold into rolls and give them the form of cake-king. Let it take another 15 minutes.

Brush the braid and the cakes-king with the yolk and place on trays, lined with vegetable paper. Take it to the oven, preheated at 180°C, for about 35 minutes. Take it back, let it cool down. Sprinkle the braid with the remaining straw almond and serve everything.

instructions: For traditional dried raisins we will need: sheet metal, grill, tulpan.
We choose grapes ripe but strong. We leave the bunches as they are, whole.
Carefully wash them and let them drain well. If the bunch is too thick, cut it into smaller pieces for faster drying.
Put the grill on top of the sheet and touch the bunches, not too close to air and melt properly.
Cover them with tulip and leave them in strong sun for several days. Sometimes, we flip the bunches to dry and dry well.
To make sure the raisin is ready, we gently shake the bunch and if the raisin falls easily then it is ready.
We chew carefully all the clusters and keep the raisin in glass sterile jars, placing intermediate leaves. Attention, the raisin must dry well because the slightest moisture will make us mold.
We can also dry it, towards the end, in the oven, at low temperature for more certainty. When ready, keep the raisin in a dark and cool cabinet.

instructions: Preheat oven to 400°. Toss pears, cherries, sugar, flour, lemon juice, cinnamon, salt, and cloves in a large bowl. Transfer to a 13x9” baking dish.

Whisk flour, baking powder, salt, and ¾ cup sugar in a medium bowl. Rub in butter with your fingers until a coarse meal forms. Gradually mix in ⅓ cup hot water until a soft wet dough forms (a few lumps are okay). Drop clumps of dough over filling; sprinkle with remaining 2 Tbsp. sugar.
Bake until filling is bubbling and top is golden brown and cooked through, 40–45 minutes; let cool. Serve with ice cream.

drumsticks-and-string-beans.name: Drumsticks And String Beans
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°. Mix yogurt and mustard in a medium bowl. Toss chicken in yogurt mixture to coat. In a large plastic bag, shake together panko, 1 tsp. salt, and pepper. Add chicken, toss until coated, and transfer to a rimmed baking sheet with a rack. Roast until chicken is golden brown and an instant-read thermometer inserted in the thickest part of the drumsticks registers 165°, 40–50 minutes. Let cool completely.

Meanwhile, cook string beans in boiling salted water until bright green and just tender, about 2 minutes. Drain beans and rinse under cold water to stop the cooking. Drain, toss with vinegar, and season with salt. Pack cold chicken and string beans in separate containers.

drunk-blondies.name: Drunk Blondies
instructions: Position a rack in the middle of the oven and preheat the oven to 177°C. Grease a 9-by-13-by-2-inch baking pan and line with parchment, allowing the ends of the paper to hang over two opposite edges of the pan.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt; set aside.

Put the butter and brown sugar in a large mixing bowl and stir with a spoon until smooth. Add the eggs, vanilla, and bourbon, and mix until thoroughly combined. Stir in the flour mixture, followed by the pecans, coconut, and chocolate chips.

Pour the batter into the prepared pan and smooth over the top with an offset spatula. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes, until the top is golden brown. Remove the pan from the oven and let cool completely on a wire rack. Cut the blondies into bars. The blondies will keep in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 3 days.

drunk-german.name: Drunk German
instructions: Pour Jagermeister into tall glass.

drunk-pears-teleculinaria.name: Drunk Pears
instructions: Light a pot with sugar, wine, spices and orange peel until the sugar dissolves.
Peel the pears, keeping their feet, and put them in the pot, taking care to be completely covered by wine.
Let them cook, in mild heat, for 30 minutes or until the pears are soft (but not undone). Drain them and serve them warm or cold.

drunk-steaks-teleculinaria.name: Drunk Steaks
instructions: Tempere os bifes com sal e pimenta. Descasque e esmague os dentes de alho. Numa frigideira com um fio de azeite, coloque os bifes, o alho e o louro e deixe fritar bem.
Water with white wine and boil until the alcohol evaporates and the sauce gets a darker color. Remove and reserve.

Peel and cut the potatoes into sticks and make them fry in hot oil. Remove, pour on absorbent paper and season with salt. Wash and cut the cherry tomatoes to taste and season with a thread of olive oil and oregano. Serve steaks accompanied by fries and tomato salad.

drunken-berries.name: Drunken Berries
instructions: Combine sugar with water in a saucepan over medium-high heat, stirring until the sugar has dissolved and the mixture comes to a boil. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes; remove from heat and let stand 5 more minutes.
Stir pear liqueur into the syrup and refrigerate for 1 hour.

Gently combine strawberries, blueberries, and blackberries in a large bowl and pour syrup over the berries to coat. Allow berries to stand for about 3 hours to absorb flavors. Mixture can stand up to 3 days if desired.

drunken-clams-and-noodles.name: Drunken Clams And Noodles

ingredients.item: -oz. packages fresh ramen noodles or four 3-oz. packages dried ramen noodles
. clams (such as littleneck or manila; about 36)

instructions: Cook noodles in a large pot of boiling water (unsalted), stirring occasionally, according to package directions. Drain noodles, reserving 2 cups noodle cooking liquid; divide noodles among bowls.
Meanwhile, heat vegetable oil in a large Dutch oven or other heavy pot over medium. Cook ginger, stirring often, until fragrant and lightly browned on both sides, about 4 minutes.

Increase heat to high and add clams, chiles, garlic, wine, sesame oil, oyster sauce, and 1½ cups reserved cooking liquid to pot. Stir to combine, then cover and cook until clams open, 5–8 minutes. Uncover pot and remove from heat. Discard any clams that haven’t opened. Stir in Thai basil. Taste broth and add more noodle cooking liquid if too salty or more oyster sauce if not salty enough. Ladle clams and broth over noodles in bowls.

drunken-grapefruit-salad.name: Drunken Grapefruit Salad
instructions: Mix grapefruit, 1/2 cup reserved juice, and sugar in a bowl until sugar dissolves. Transfer grapefruit mixture to 8 serving cups; top each with about 1 teaspoon gin. Sprinkle minced mint over each cup. Garnish each cup with 1 mint leaf.

Add the fruit to the syrup and boil for 15 minutes.
Then remove the fruits, let them cool a little and remove the peel.
Boil the syrup for about 10 minutes until it thickens.
Let the syrup cool and then remove the cinnamon from the inside.
We pour the vanillas and freeze them.
We serve them in high glasses.

drupey-pie.name: Drupey Pie
instructions: Preheat oven to 218°C (220 degrees C). Place cherries, nectarines and apricots into a colander to drain off any excess liquid.

To Make Dough: In a medium bowl, combine flour and salt. Cut 3/4 cup of butter into flour mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs. Pour in one tablespoon of water at a time until the dough sticks together. Add additional water if necessary. Divide dough into two parts. Roll out one half toa circle 1/8 of an inch thick, then place in a 9 inch pie pan. Roll out the other half of the dough and cut into long strips about 1/2 inch wide. Set aside.

To Make Filling: Place the drained fruit mixture into a medium bowl, Stir in the sugar, cinnamon, cornstarch and flour; pour into the prepared pie pan. Dot with the remaining two tablespoons of butter. Arrange the pastry strips into a lattice top and crimp edges. In a small bowl, whisk together the egg yolk and teaspoon of water, and use a pastry brush to coat just the lattice strips--not the edges.

Bake in the preheated oven and bake for 25 minutes, until the crust begins to brown. Reduce the heat to 177°C (175 degrees C) and bake for an additional 20 to 25 minutes. Remove from oven and let cool a bit before serving.

Flip turkey breast-side down, cover with plastic wrap, and refrigerate for about 1 more day.

Remove plastic wrap and place turkey on a rack over a baking sheet the night before you plan to roast. Allow turkey to air-dry in the refrigerator for at least 8 hours. This step helps the skin crisp beautifully during roasting.
Bring turkey to room temperature, 1 to 2 hours; pat dry with paper towels. Season turkey with black pepper and place onion and celery in the cavity.
Preheat oven to 218°C (220 degrees C). Place turkey, breast-side down, in a roasting pan.

Roast in the preheated oven until skin is golden, about 30 minutes. Remove turkey from oven and flip to breast-side up. Reduce oven temperature to 163°C (165 degrees C); continue roasting until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part of the thigh reads 74°C (74 degrees C), about 2 hours.
Transfer turkey to a large platter and loosely tent with aluminum foil; allow turkey to rest about 30 minutes before carving.

dry-chimichurri-spice-rub.name: Dry Chimichurri Spice Rub

ingredients.item: To 2 teaspoons dried crushed red pepper
* sometimes labeled pimentón dulce or pimentón de la vera dulce; available at some supermarkets

instructions:
Whisk all ingredients in medium bowl. Transfer to airtight container.

dry-martini-argiro.name: Dry Martini

ingredients.item: 10-15 mlextra dry or simple dry white dry vermouth
Green olives passed on bamboo stick
Lemon peel or lime for garnishing
10-15 mlextra dry or simple dry white dry vermouth
Green olives passed on bamboo stick
Lemon peel or lime for garnishing

instructions: The recipe for dry martini cocktails consists of two main ingredients, gin (longon dry type) or vodka and dry vermouth in proportions differentiated according to consumer preferences.
A typical dry martini contains 70 ml London dry Gin or Vodka and a few drops or 10 ml of very dry vermouth type extra dry.
Mix the ingredients of the recipe with a barspoon in a glass stirring container or even better metal for 30 seconds.
Strain in a cold Martini glass (cocktail glass).
A second way of executing the recipe is by hitting a Shaker filled with lots of ice all ingredients for 20 seconds. It's good if we prefer to enjoy our martini with gin, avoid using the Shaker.
Garnish with green olives that we pin on a bamboo stick, or alternatively with a lemon peel or lime, after spraying its oils over our cocktail.

Depending on the consumer's preferences, with regard to the content of gin or dry vermouth, there are the following versions. For all versions we follow the same way of execution as described in the classic recipe.
Extra dry Martini 70 ml London dry gin or vodka 5 ml extra dry vermouth 1 drop aromatic orange bitters (optional) olives green or lemon peel or lime for garnishing
Wet Martini 50 ml London dry gin or Vodka 50 ml white dry vermouth type extra dry 1 drop aromatic orange bitters (optional) olives green or lemon peel or lime for garnishing

Perfect Martini 60 ml London dry gin or Vodka 10 ml white dry vermouth type extra dry or dry 10 ml sweet vermouth white or red 1 drop aromatic orange bitters (optional) olives green or lemon peel or lime for garnishing
Upside down Martini 60 ml white dry vermouth type extra dry or dry 40 ml London dry gin or Vodka 1 drop aromatic orange bitters (optional) olives green or lemon peel or lime for garnishing
Dirty Martini 70 ml London dry gin or Vodka 10-15 ml extra dry or simple dry white dry dry Vermouth 10 ml green olives (optional) green olives passed on bamboo stick for garnishing

instructions: Stir onion flakes, beef bouillon granules, onion powder, parsley flakes, celery seed, paprika, and black pepper together in a bowl. Use as a substitute for a 1-ounce envelope of dry onion soup mix.

dry-rub.name: Dry Rub
instructions:
Stir peppercorns, mustard seeds, and cumin seeds in a small skillet over medium heat until toasted, about 2 minutes. Let cool. Put into a spice grinder with next 6 ingredients and pulse until finely ground.
Do ahead: Store in an airtight container for up to 3 months.

dry-stir-fried-green-beans.name: Dry Stir-fried Green Beans
instructions: Heat the cooking oil in a large frying pan or wok on high heat until very hot. Add the green beans and cook for 4-5 minutes, until the beans blister on one side. Turn the beans over and cook for another 2-3 minutes. Try not to move the beans around too much during cooking; they should be slightly wrinkled and softened. Using a slotted spoon, remove the beans from the pan.

In the same pan, add a little more oil if it’s a bit dry. Turn the heat down slightly; add the garlic, green onion, and chili; and cook until fragrant, about 1-2 minutes, taking care not to burn the garlic.
Toss the beans back in and add the salt, sugar, and MSG, if using, and mix thoroughly. Check the seasoning and add more to taste, if needed.

dry-stuffing-with-olive-oil-lezzet.name: Dry Stuffing With Olive Oil

ingredients.item: 1 kg of dry pepper and eggplant

5. Add 1 cup of hot water and brew off the cover.

dry-aged-prime-rib.name: Dry-aged Prime Rib
instructions: Dissolve 2 teaspoons kosher salt in 1/2 cup water. Wipe down prime rib with kosher salt water mixture.

Pour enough sea salt over a rimmed baking sheet until completely covered; sprinkle Himalayan pink salt on top. Place a roasting rack over salt on baking sheet. Place prime rib on the rack and refrigerate at 34 to 3°C (1 to 3 degrees C), uncovered, for 30 to 40 days.

Remove prime rib from the refrigerator. Trim fat as needed. Transfer the rack into a roasting pan; place prime rib on top. Spray prime rib with water; season generously with kosher salt. Refrigerate 24 to 48 hours to let meat absorb salt.
Remove prime rib from the refrigerator; cover loosely with aluminum foil until warmed up slightly, 1 1/2 to 2 hours.
Preheat the oven to 260°C (260 degrees C). Insert a probe thermometer into the prime rib.

Roast in the preheated oven for 20 minutes. Reduce the oven temperature to 149°C (150 degrees C). Bake until desired doneness is reached, about 90 minutes. [52°C (52 degrees C) for rare, 54°C (54 degrees C) for medium-rare, or 57°C (57 degrees C) for medium] Rest prime rib before slicing, 30 minutes.

dry-brined-roast-turkey.name: Dry-brined Roast Turkey

ingredients.item: Diamond crystal or 7 tablespoons morton kosher salt

ingredients.prep: neck and giblets removed, patted dry

instructions: Place salt and brown sugar in a medium bowl and work together with your fingers to incorporate. Place turkey on a flat or V-shape roasting rack set inside a large roasting pan. Pack dry brine all over turkey, inside and out, nudging some into areas where the skin naturally separates from the bird, such as around the neck and top of the breast and between the legs and breast. Chill, uncovered, at least 12 hours and up to 2 days.

Place oven rack in lower third of oven; preheat to 425°. Rinse turkey and pat dry. Rinse roasting pan and rack if needed. Place turkey, breast side up, on rack in roasting pan and pour 1 cup water into pan. This will prevent drippings from burning. Roast turkey, rotating pan back to front halfway through and adding more water by ½-cupfuls as needed to maintain some liquid in the pan, until skin is browned all over, 35–45 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook rosemary, garlic, butter, and soy sauce in a small saucepan over medium heat until bubbling and fragrant, about 5 minutes; keep warm.

Reduce oven temperature to 350° and continue to roast turkey, basting with butter mixture every 10–15 minutes and rotating pan every 30 minutes or so if bird is browning unevenly, until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part of breast registers 150° (temperature will continue to climb as the bird rests), 40–70 minutes longer. Transfer turkey to a cutting board and let rest at least 30 minutes and up to 1 hour before carving.

dry-brined-turkey.name: Dry-brined Turkey

ingredients.prep: Garlic and Herb Dry Brine, or Fennel, Chile, and Maple Dry Brine

instructions: Rub dry brine all over turkey; chill uncovered, 6–7 hours.
Preheat oven to 218°C (218°C).
Rinse turkey under cold water; pat dry and place, breast side up, on a rack set in a large roasting pan.
Stuff turkey with onion, garlic, and herbs.
Working from neck end of turkey, gently loosen skin from breasts and rub butter under skin and all over outside of bird.
Tie legs together with kitchen twine, pour broth into pan, and roast turkey for 30 minutes.

Reduce oven temperature to 163°C (163°C) and roast, basting with pan juices every 30–40 minutes, adding more broth as needed to maintain some liquid in pan, and tenting with foil if skin is browning too quickly, until an instant-read thermometer inserted into thickest part of thigh registers 74°C (74°C), for 2½–3 hours.
Transfer turkey to a platter; tent with foil.
Let rest at least 30 minutes before carving.

dry-brined-turkey-with-tangy-honey-glaze.name: Dry-brined Turkey With Tangy Honey Glaze

ingredients.item: Diamond crystal or  0.25 cup plus 1 0.5 tsp. morton kosher salt

ingredients.prep: neck reserved for gravy, giblets discarded, patted dry

ingredients.item: Sherry vinegar or red wine vinegar

instructions: Place ½ cup Diamond Crystal or ¼ cup plus 1½ tsp. Morton kosher salt and 1 Tbsp. light brown sugar in a small bowl and work together with your fingers until incorporated. Place one 12–14-lb. turkey, neck reserved for gravy, giblets discarded, on a wire rack set inside a rimmed baking sheet and pat dry with paper towels. (If you don’t have this setup, place your turkey on a V-shape rack set inside a large roasting pan.) Sprinkle salt mixture all over outside of the turkey as well as inside the cavity of the turkey, patting to adhere and nudging some into every crevice. You won’t need all of the dry brine, but it’s good to have extra since some of it will end up on the baking sheet as you season the turkey. Chill bird, uncovered, at least 12 hours and up to 2 days.

Remove turkey from wire rack and rinse baking sheet and rack if needed (turkey will most likely release some liquid onto pan). Line baking sheet with 3 layers of foil and set rack back inside. Place turkey, breast side up, on rack and tuck wings underneath. Let turkey sit at room temperature 2–3 hours.

Place an oven rack in middle of oven; preheat oven to 450°. Using your fingers, loosen turkey skin on breast. Work 4 Tbsp. unsalted butter, room temperature, under skin, spreading evenly over both breasts. Smear outside of turkey with another 4 Tbsp. unsalted butter, room temperature.
Tie legs together with kitchen twine and pour 1 cup water into baking sheet.
Roast turkey, rotating pan halfway through, until skin is mostly golden brown all over, about 30 minutes.

Meanwhile, cook ¼ cup sherry vinegar or red wine vinegar, 2 Tbsp. honey, 4 tsp. Worcestershire sauce, 3 sprigs rosemary, 3 garlic cloves, crushed, two 2x1" strips orange zest, and remaining 4 Tbsp. unsalted butter, room temperature, in a small saucepan over medium heat until bubbling and slightly thickened, about 5 minutes. Reduce heat to lowest setting and keep glaze warm.

Reduce oven temperature to 300° and continue to roast turkey, brushing with glaze every 30 minutes and adding more water by ½-cupfuls as needed to maintain some liquid in baking sheet, until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part of breast near the neck registers 155° (don’t worry; the temperature will continue to climb while the bird rests), 65–85 minutes longer. Skin should be deep golden brown, shiny, and crisp. Transfer turkey to a cutting board and let rest at least 30 minutes and up to 1 hour before carving.
Editor’s note: The dry-brine turkey recipe was first printed in our November 2018 issue as ‘Dry-Rubbed Roast Turkey.’ Head this way for more of our best Thanksgiving recipes →

dry-roasted-brown-butter-onions.name: Dry-roasted Brown Butter Onions
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°. Cut white onions, larger cipolline onions, and shallots almost in half, leaving root ends intact. Pry each open slightly to expose cut sides. Place white onions root side down in a foil-lined roasting pan and roast until just beginning to soften and brown, 75-90 minutes.
Add cipolline onions and shallots to roasting pan root end down and roast until all onions and shallots are soft and deeply colored, about 1 hour longer.

Just before onions finish cooking, melt butter in a small saucepan and cook, swirling occasionally, until butter is brown and smells nutty, 5-8 minutes. Let cool slightly; whisk in vinegar and season brown butter with salt.
Divide onions and shallots among plates, leaving skins on; spoon brown butter over.

dry-rubbed-flank-steak-with-grilled-corn.name: Dry-rubbed Flank Steak With Grilled Corn Salsa
instructions: Combine brown sugar, chile powder, paprika, salt, pepper, cayenne, granulated garlic, mustard powder, coriander, and cumin in a small bowl.

Prepare a grill for medium-high heat; oil grate. Grill corn, turning occasionally, until lightly browned all over, 8–10 minutes; let cool. Cut kernels from cobs and place in a medium bowl. Add onion, jalapeño, tomatoes, cilantro, and lime juice to corn and toss to combine; season with salt and pepper. Set salsa aside.

Meanwhile, coat steak with all of dry rub, packing on more than once if needed, and drizzle with 2 Tbsp. oil to help rub adhere. Grill steak, turning occasionally and moving to a cooler spot on grill as needed to control flare-ups, until nicely browned and an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part registers 130°, about 4 minutes per side for medium-rare. Transfer to a cutting board and let rest 10 minutes.
Return steak to grill just to recrisp exterior, about 1 minute per side. Transfer back to cutting board and slice against the grain. Serve topped with salsa.
Do Ahead: Salsa can be made 1 day ahead. Cover and chill.

dry-rubbed-pork-roast.name: Dry-rubbed Pork Roast

ingredients.item: . diamond crystal or 4 0.5 tsp. morton kosher salt

ingredients.prep: preferably skin-on and with fat cap, chine bone removed

instructions: Preheat oven to 500°. Coarsely grind bay leaves, peppercorns, coriander seeds, and fennel seeds in a spice mill, mortar and pestle, or blender. Transfer spice mixture to a small bowl and mix in rosemary, kosher salt, and 2 Tbsp. oil.

Using a sharp knife, score fat cap in a crosshatch pattern, spacing cuts about ½" apart and avoiding cutting into flesh. Rub spice mixture all over pork, making sure to work it into the cuts. Set pork on a wire rack set inside a roasting pan.

Toss onions, garlic, and remaining 1 Tbsp. oil in a medium bowl to coat, then scatter around pork. Roast in oven until skin begins to puff and crisp, about 30 minutes. Remove roast from oven and reduce oven temperature to 350°. Pour wine and 1 cup water into pan and return to oven. Roast until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the center of roast registers 150°, 45–75 minutes (time will vary depending on how thick your roast is). Let rest at least 30 minutes and up to 2 hours before carving.
Sprinkle roast with sea salt and serve with any pan juices spooned over.
Do Ahead: Pork can be rubbed with spice mixture 2 days ahead. Cover; chill.

dry-rubbed-roast-turkey.name: Dry-rubbed Roast Turkey

ingredients.item: Diamond crystal or 1/4 cup plus 1 1/2 tsp. morton kosher salt

ingredients.prep: neck reserved for gravy, giblets discarded, patted dry

ingredients.item: Sherry vinegar or red wine vinegar

instructions: Place salt and brown sugar in a medium bowl and work together with your fingers until incorporated. Place turkey on a wire rack set inside a rimmed baking sheet. (If you don’t have this setup, place your turkey on a V-shape rack set inside a large roasting pan.) Sprinkle dry brine all over outside and inside of turkey, patting to adhere and nudging some into crevices. You won’t need all of the dry brine, but it’s good to have extra since some of it will end up on the baking sheet as you season the turkey. Chill bird, uncovered, at least 12 hours and up to 2 days.

Remove turkey from wire rack and rinse baking sheet and rack if needed (turkey will most likely release some liquid onto pan). Line baking sheet with 3 layers of foil and set rack back inside. Place turkey, breast side up, on rack and tuck wings underneath. Let turkey sit at room temperature 2–3 hours.

Place an oven rack in middle of oven; preheat to 232°C. Using your fingers, loosen skin on breast. Work 4 Tbsp. butter under skin, spreading evenly over both breasts. Smear outside of turkey with another 4 Tbsp. butter.
Tie legs together with kitchen twine and pour 1 cup water into baking sheet. Roast turkey, rotating pan halfway through, until skin is mostly golden brown all over, about 30 minutes.

Meanwhile, cook vinegar, honey, Worcestershire sauce, rosemary, garlic, orange zest, and remaining 4 Tbsp. butter in a small saucepan over medium heat until bubbling and slightly thickened, about 5 minutes. Reduce heat to lowest setting and keep glaze warm.

Reduce oven temperature to 149°C and continue to roast turkey, brushing with glaze every 30 minutes and adding more water by 1/2-cupfuls as needed to maintain some liquid in baking sheet, until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part of breast near the neck registers 66°C (don’t worry; the temperature will continue to climb while the bird rests), 65–85 minutes longer. Skin should be deep golden brown, shiny, and crisp. Transfer turkey to a cutting board and let rest at least 30 minutes and up to 1 hour before carving.

dry-rubbed-turkey-breast.name: Dry-rubbed Turkey Breast

ingredients.item: Diamond crystal or  0.25 cup morton kosher salt

instructions: Coarsely grind coriander seeds, fennel seeds, and peppercorns in spice mill or with mortar and pestle. (If you don’t have either, you can always chop with a chef’s knife.) Place salt and brown sugar in a medium bowl and work together with your fingers until incorporated. Stir in ground spices.

Place turkey on a wire rack set inside a rimmed baking sheet. (If you don’t have this setup, place your turkey on a V-shape rack set inside a large roasting pan.) Sprinkle dry brine all over both sides of turkey breast, patting to adhere. You won’t need all of the dry brine, but it’s good to have extra since some of it will end up on the baking sheet as you season the turkey breast. Chill, uncovered, at least 12 hours and up to 2 days.

Remove turkey breast from wire rack and rinse baking sheet and rack if needed (turkey will most likely release some liquid onto pan). Line baking sheet with 3 layers of foil and set rack back inside. Place turkey breast back on rack and let sit at room temperature 2–3 hours.
Place an oven rack in middle of oven; preheat to 450°. Pour 1 cup water into baking sheet.
Roast turkey breast, rotating pan halfway through, until skin is mostly golden brown all over, about 30 minutes.

Reduce oven temperature to 300° and continue to roast turkey breast, adding more water by ½-cupfuls as needed to maintain some liquid in baking sheet, until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part of breast registers 150° (don’t worry; the temperature will continue to climb while the turkey breast rests), 50–70 minutes longer. Skin should be deep golden brown and crisp. Transfer turkey breast to a cutting board and let rest at least 30 minutes and up to 1 hour before slicing.

dsfs-pretzel-bread.name: Dsf's Pretzel Bread
instructions: Stir dark brown sugar, 1 tablespoon honey, and yeast into warm water in a large bowl, dissolving sugar and yeast. Let stand until a foamy layer forms on the water, about 10 minutes. Stir 3 tablespoons melted butter, half-and-half, and salt into yeast mixture.

Beat 1 cup flour to wet ingredients, forming a wet, sloppy dough. Turn dough out onto a floured work surface and gradually knead remaining 1 cup flour into dough until dough is only slightly sticky, about 10 more minutes. Add more flour if dough is too wet. Form dough into a ball.

Grease a large bowl with 1 teaspoon butter; place dough ball into bowl and turn to lightly coat with butter. Cover bowl with plastic wrap and let rise in a warm area until doubled, 1 hour to 1 hour and 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 232°C (230 degrees C). Spray a baking sheet with cooking spray.

Stir baking soda and 3 tablespoons honey (if you like a sweeter crust) into a large pot of water and bring to a full rolling boil. Punch down dough and divide in half. Form each half into a small loaf. Carefully drop a loaf into the boiling water and cook for 30 seconds on each side. Dough should float. Remove loaf gently and let excess water drip off; place loaf on prepared baking sheet. Boil the second loaf, following same steps, and place onto baking sheet.
Sprinkle each loaf lightly with salt. Use a sharp knife to cut an X shape into the top of each loaf.

Bake on a center rack in preheated oven for 10 minutes. Reduce oven temperature to 204°C (205 degrees C) and continue baking until crust is shiny and browned and loaves are done in the middle, 10 more minutes. For a softer crust, brush tops of loaves with melted butter.

dsfs-smoke-and-ginger.name: Dsf's Smoke And Ginger
instructions: Fill a shaker with ice; add ginger liqueur and Scotch. Cover shaker and shake; pour into a tumbler and top with ginger beer.

dublin-iced-coffee.name: Dublin Iced Coffee

ingredients.prep: such as alesmith speedway or guinness

instructions: Mix coffee, stout, whiskey, and simple syrup in a highball glass. Add ice to fill. Gently pour in cream so it gradually sinks into coffee; sprinkle with cinnamon.

duchess-baked-potatoes.name: Duchess Baked Potatoes
instructions: Place potatoes in a large pot and pour in water to cover by 2"; season with salt. Bring to a boil over medium-high. Reduce heat and simmer until a paring knife very easily slides through potatoes, 25–35 minutes. Drain and let cool slightly.

Preheat oven to 425°. Whisk egg yolks, garlic, cream, sour cream, butter, and peppercorns in a large bowl; season generously with salt. Peel potatoes and pass flesh through a ricer or a food mill (or mash them by hand if you don’t mind a few lumps) directly into bowl with egg mixture. Fold in gently (be careful not to overmix). Transfer to a 3-quart baking dish and decoratively shingle or swirl surface. Bake, rotating once, until golden brown and slightly puffed, 30–40 minutes.
Do Ahead: Dish can be assembled 1 day ahead. Cover and chill. Increase bake time by 5–10 minutes.

duchess-potatoes-recipe-step-by-step-com.name: Duchess Potatoes Recipe Step By Step

instructions: Pela las papas y corta en cubos.
Put them in a pot and cook them till they're tender. That'll take about 20 minutes or more.
Escurre muy bien las patatas y deja secar un rato para eliminar cualquier exceso de agua.
Put the dried potatoes into a bowl and tighten till purée forms.
Derrite la mantequilla en el microondas y agrega a las patatas.
Mezcla muy bien hasta que quede bien suave.
Incorpora las yemas de huevo, sal fina al gusto, la pizca de nuez moscada y pimienta.
Mezcla suavemente hasta que todo se integre.
Once ready, move purée to a pastry sleeve with a curly nozzle.
Cubre una bandeja con papel encerado o de hornear.
Give them a pink shape with a pastry sleeve or put them up to your best.
When all are formed, purge at 200 ″ 3 ″ for 15-20 minutes till they are golden.
Retira del horno y disfruta caliente o tibio.

duck-and-mushroom-risotto-teleculinaria.name: Duck And Mushroom Risotto
instructions: Start the oven at 180 degrees. Season the duck legs with salt, pepper and lemon zest. Reserve.

Heat a non-stick skillet with lid. Put the duck legs in it with the fur facing down and let them cook until golden. Turn them over and let them cook equally on this side. Cover the pan and put it in the oven for about an hour. Take it off, let it dampen and shred the flesh.

Light a pot with the olive oil thread and the chopped onion and let it saute. Add the rice, stir well and let cook a little. Add the white wine, stir and let it reduce in full. Then add some vegetable broth and let it cook in very mild heat. Whenever the rice absorbs the liquid in full, add a little more broth and let it cook until the grain is almost open (about 15 to 20 minutes).

Numa frigideira, deite as 2 colheres (sopa) de azeite e os cogumelos, grosseiramente picados, e deixe saltear. Tempere com sal e pimenta, mexa e junte ao arroz, bem como parte do pato desfiado. Envolva bem.
Add also the butter and grated parmesan cheese, mix and retrieve the seasonings. Transfer to a plate, sprinkle with more grated parmesan cheese and serve with the rest of the duck.

duck-and-mushroom-risotto-recipe-comeder.name: Duck And Mushroom Risotto Recipe
duck-and-mushroom-risotto-recipe-comeder.original_name: Receta de risotto de pato y setas
instructions: He scattering into small portions the duck magret.
Pela los dientes de ajo y filetea finamente. Corta en cuadritos la cebolla blanca.
Calienta una cazuela con un poco de aceite de oliva y dora los dientes de ajo junto a la cebolla.
Incorpora el magret de pato fileteado, mezcla y sella por 5 minutos hasta que dore.
Mientras, rebana las setas y limpia muy bien.
When the magret's a little golden, add mushrooms and salt and pepper to suit. Meg and cook for a few minutes.
Agrega el arroz y mezcla. Vierte el vino blanco y cocina hasta que el alcohol se evapore.
Once the liquid has been reduced, pour some of the stock and cook the risotto over medium heat while still moving.
Debes ir agregando progresivamente el caldo e ir cocinando a fuego medio mientras sin dejar de mezclar.
Cuando tengas esa textura cremosa, incorpora la cucharada de mantequilla y el queso parmesano, mezcla hasta integrar.
Serves and enjoys with more parmesan and cilantro cheese.
Enjoy!

duck-bigarade.name: Duck Bigarade

ingredients.item: Basic duck stock or chicken stock
Shot glass grand marnier or other orange liqueur

instructions: Remove the duck breasts from the refrigerator, salt them well, and set them aside at room temperature for 30 minutes.

Pan sear the duck breasts. You may have to do this in batches. When the breasts are cooked, set them aside skin side up on a cutting board and let them rest, tented with aluminum foil, while you make the sauce.

To make the sauce, pour off all but about 2 tablespoons of the fat from the pan and place the pan over medium heat. Sprinkle the flour into the pan and stir to combine and make a roux. Let it cook, stirring occasionally, for 4 to 5 minutes, until it is the color of coffee with cream.

Add a pinch of salt and stir to combine, then slowly stir in the stock, orange juice, liqueur, and vinegar. Everything will spatter at first, but it will calm down. Add any accumulated juices from the duck to the sauce. Let this boil down until it is a little thinner than the consistency of Thanksgiving gravy. Add the sugar, then taste and adjust with salt. If you want a more refined sauce, pour it through a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl.
To serve, slice the breasts. Spoon some sauce on each plate and top with breast slices. Garnish with the orange zest and orange slices.

duck-breast-zucchini-tournedos.name: Duck Breast & Zucchini Tournedos

ingredients.item: Dried cep mushrooms ('champagne cork' or bouchon) or porcini

instructions: For the mushroom powder, process the dried cep mushrooms until finely ground, combine them with the pepper and herbes de Provençe.
Cut the duck breasts lengthways into 3 mm (⅛ inch) wide strips.

Lay out the strips of duck two by two on top of each other, alternating layers of fat and lean. Cut the zucchini into strips using a vegetable peeler. Lay the strips of zucchini on top of the duck, season with the mushroom powder and season with salt. Roll up the strips tightly to make tournedos and tie with string. Make 6 tournedos in this way. Cook over gentle heat 5 minutes each side.

duck-breast-salad-teleculinaria.name: Duck Breast Salad

ingredients.item: Sal e pimenta preta de moinho q.b.

instructions: Wash the duck breasts, dry them with absorbent paper and make some cross cuts on the fat. Season them with salt, port wine and lemon scrape.
Leve a lume alto uma frigideira antiaderente e deixe aquecer. Junte a carne, com a gordura virada para baixo, deixe-a corar bem, de ambos os lados, e depois retire-a.
Wash the arugula, drain it and dispose of it on a large plate. Slice the duck breasts and lay them on the arugula. Remove the berries from the pomegranate and spread them across the salad.
In a bowl, mix some salt and pepper with olive oil, balsamic vinegar and lemon juice. Water the salad with this sauce, add the croutons, sprinkle with herb of provenance and serve.

duck-breast-with-mustard-greens-turnips-.name: Duck Breast With Mustard Greens, Turnips, And Radishes

ingredients.item: Kosher salt and freshly ground pepper

ingredients.prep: scrubbed, thinly sliced, plus 2 cups torn turnip greens or kale

ingredients.item: Torn mustard greens; plus any mustard flowers

instructions: Preheat oven to 400°. Score fat side of duck breasts ⅛" deep in a crosshatch pattern; season both sides with kosher salt and pepper. Heat 1 Tbsp. vegetable oil in a large ovenproof skillet, preferably cast iron, over medium. Cook 2 duck breasts, skin side down, until fat is rendered and surface is deeply browned and crisp, 10–15 minutes; transfer to a plate. Wipe out skillet and repeat with remaining duck and 1 Tbsp. vegetable oil.

Arrange all duck breasts in skillet, fat side up, and roast in oven until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the center of breasts registers 135°, 5–8 minutes. Transfer to a cutting board and let rest at least 5 minutes and up to 2 hours.
Whisk Dijon mustard, mustard powder, lemon juice, and 3 Tbsp. olive oil in a small bowl; season mustard sauce with kosher salt and pepper.
Toss radishes, turnips, greens, flowers (if using), vinegar, and remaining 2 Tbsp. olive oil in a large bowl; season with kosher salt and pepper.
Thinly slice duck. Scatter greens over a platter (or two) and top with duck. Sprinkle with sea salt and serve with mustard sauce alongside.

duck-breast-with-orange-sauce-teleculina.name: Duck Breast With Orange Sauce
duck-breast-with-orange-sauce-teleculina.original_name: Peito de pato com molho de laranja

ingredients.item: Arroz de nozes e passas para acompanhar

instructions: Light the water with the juice and the oranges, the orange liqueur, the sugar, the cloves, the thyme and the laurel. When boiling, lower the heat and let it clear for 20 minutes, stirring from time to time. Coe by a dealer and book.
Descasque e pique os alhos e a chalota e aloure no azeite. Junte os peitos de pato, previamente temperados com sal e pimenta, e deixe dourar de ambos os lados. Retire e reserve.
